content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test URL, discovery, parser options, unknown validation, normalization, schedule, cursor, state, and policy with injected clocks and ports.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Deterministic Unit Ports model · Theory Feed Ts…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Deterministic Unit Ports precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Deterministic Unit Ports model · Theory Feed Ts…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 2491 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile original fixtures, deterministic async tests, compile-negative cases, and evidence non-claims; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test URL, discovery, parser options, unknown validation, normalization, schedule, cursor, state, and policy with injected clocks and ports.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Deterministic Unit Ports model · Theory Feed Ts…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Backend value comes only from full integration tests.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: feed-ts-deterministic-unit-ports-156573.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-ts-deterministic-unit-ports-156573(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_feed_ts_deterministic_unit_ports_156573\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?testCases < 16)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?passingCases < 16 \|\| passingCases > testCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?compileNegativeCases < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsupportedClaims > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using test and discovery plus specific case evidence.
